generated_comment: Yaar honestly, I don't mind supporting women, but office policy bhi fair honi chahiye na. Aajkal menstrual leave, then 6 months maternity, phir childcare flexibility—company ko project delivery bhi dekhna hota hai, and men end up covering extra work without any such benefit.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women's biological and caregiving needs as an unfair workplace liability while presenting the concern as neutral fairness. It encodes reverse-discrimination and productivity/cost arguments against menstrual and maternity accommodations.
original_frame: Motherhood as Basis of Respect, Women Defined by Male Relations
persona_text: You are Anita. Age 39. Married with two children. A working woman in Delhi. You are at your office and a younger female colleague named Shreya who is 31 and single has just been announced as the new team head. You are on a WhatsApp call with your closest friend after office hours. You are talking about Shreya's promotion and what you think of her as a person and a leader and in the course of the conversation you reveal that you do not fully respect Shreya as a woman because she has no husband and no children and you are not sure what kind of decisions she can make for a team when she has no real responsibilities at home.
